verdict.summary: Guide is broadly useful, but it contains a likely wrong TMRG normal-priority deadline, uses outdated Lisbon hospital names, and overstates the scope/source of the oncology referral network.

issues.current: «Юридические TMRG для первой больничной консультации: 30 дней для muito prioritária, 60 для prioritária, 120 для normal.» / «Primeira consulta de especialidade hospitalar normal: до 120 календарных дней»
issues.problem: For a first hospital specialty consultation referred through SNS/CTH/SIGA, the normal-priority TMRG is commonly stated in the legal TMRG table as 150 consecutive days, not 120. The 30/60 values are correct, but the normal value appears wrong.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: «Primeira consulta de especialidade hospitalar: muito prioritária — 30 dias; prioritária — 60 dias; normal — 150 dias, counted from registration of the valid request in SIGA/CTH, unless a more specific oncology pathway/TMRG applies.»
issues.source: Portaria n.º 153/2017, Anexo — Tempos Máximos de Resposta Garantidos; ePortugal TMRG

issues.current: «Rede de Referenciação Hospitalar de Oncologia Médica — это официальная карта потоков пациентов между SNS-больницами… Сеть учитывает тип опухоли, стадию, необходимость cirurgia complexa, radioterapia, medicina nuclear, hematologia, трансплантации…»
issues.problem: The text treats «Rede de Referenciação Hospitalar de Oncologia Médica» as if it were the complete national oncology routing map for all cancer care. Its formal scope is medical oncology; surgery, radiotherapy, haematology/haemato-oncology, transplantation and some highly specialised procedures may be governed by other referral rules, centres of reference, local ULS pathways or disease-specific arrangements.
issues.suggestion: Narrow the wording: «A RRH de Oncologia Médica is an official reference framework for medical oncology referrals, but the actual cancer pathway may also depend on tumour-specific rules, surgical/radiotherapy/haematology capacity, ULS protocols and centres of reference. Do not assume one single “oncology network” determines every step.»
issues.source: Rede de Referenciação Hospitalar de Oncologia Médica; ACSS/DGS/SNS referral network documentation

issues.current: «CHULN / Hospital de Santa Maria, CHLO, ULS São José» / «CHULN / Hospital de Santa Maria лечит…»
issues.problem: CHULN and CHLO are outdated official institutional names after the 2024 ULS reorganisation. They may still be used colloquially, but official references should use the current ULS names.
issues.suggestion: Write: «ULS Santa Maria / Hospital de Santa Maria (ex-CHULN), ULS Lisboa Ocidental (ex-CHLO), ULS São José…». In lists, keep old acronyms only as aliases for searchability.

issues.current: «muito prioritário / P1 или через Alerta-Oncológico» / «P1 / Alerta-Oncológico — практический ускоренный канал для suspeita oncológica»
issues.problem: The wording risks implying that P1/Alerta-Oncológico is a universal national legal category equivalent to the TMRG priority. In practice, oncology alert channels and labels can be local/regional or system-specific; the legally relevant point for TMRG is the priority registered in SIGA/CTH and the validated request date.
issues.suggestion: Clarify: «Some ULS/hospitals use oncology-alert workflows or P1-style labels, but availability and naming are not uniform. For enforceable deadlines, check the actual SIGA/CTH priority: muito prioritária, prioritária or normal, and the registration/acceptance date.»

issues.current: Sources include SNS PNDO, ePortugal TMRG, ERS complaints and Portaria n.º 153/2017, but no source for the Rede de Referenciação Hospitalar de Oncologia Médica itself.
issues.problem: The guide makes detailed claims about official oncology referral networks but does not cite the official referral network document/page. For a healthcare-routing guide, that is a material missing citation.
issues.suggestion: Add an official source specifically for «Rede de Referenciação Hospitalar de Oncologia Médica» or the current SNS/DE-SNS/ACSS referral-network page, and distinguish it from the broader Programa Nacional para as Doenças Oncológicas.
issues.source: ACSS/DGS/SNS — Rede de Referenciação Hospitalar de Oncologia Médica

issues.problem: AIMA is unrelated to the article topic. This looks like a taxonomy/copy-paste error and can confuse search or recommendations.
issues.suggestion: Remove «aima» and replace with a relevant tag such as «ULS», «MGF», «CTH», «SIGA» or «ERS».

missing_topics: Explain that oncology-specific TMRG may also matter after first consultation, especially for diagnostic exams, surgery and start of treatment; the guide currently focuses almost only on first hospital consultation.

Mention that TMRG complaints should include the exact SIGA/CTH request date, triage/acceptance date if known, priority level and proof of referral; without these, ERS/Livro de Reclamações complaints are weaker.

Add a caveat for migrants without an assigned médico de família: entry may be through the Centro de Saúde/USF doctor on duty, atendimento complementar, SNS 24 or urgência depending on severity, not only the named MGF.
Add the official Livro de Reclamações Eletrónico URL as a source or practical link, alongside ERS.
